title: 1.1: Reports the health endpoint HTTP response with the X-Minio-Write-Quorum header showing the required disk count (e.g., X-Minio-Write-Quorum: 3).
1.2: Reports a successful write operation while one disk is offline (e.g., upload progress '100.00%' or 'Bucket created successfully').
1.3: Reports a failed write operation with observed quorum-related error output (e.g., 'Write quorum not met' or 'Unable to upload') when too many disks are offline.
1.4: Reports observed log output when a disk goes offline (e.g., 'taking drive /path/to/disk offline') that includes the full disk path.
1.5: Reports the log message pattern when a disk comes back online automatically (e.g., 'Read/Write/Delete successful, bringing drive /path/to/disk online').
1.6: States that MinIO polls offline disks at a regular interval (e.g., every 5 seconds) to detect recovery.
1.7: Identifies the quorum calculation function (e.g., defaultWQuorum in cmd/erasure.go) as the location where write quorum is determined.
1.8: Reports that the health endpoint changes its response when write quorum cannot be met (e.g., returns an error status or indicates unhealthy state).
1.9: Explains that objects written during degraded state are automatically queued for healing (e.g., via the MRF system, healing queue, or background healing process).
